title: Treasury Releases Two New Resources to Guide AI Use in the Financial Sector
summary: The U.S. Treasury released two documents on February 19, 2026—a shared Artificial Intelligence Lexicon and the Financial Services AI Risk Management Framework (FS AI RMF)—to guide AI adoption in the financial sector. Developed through the Financial and Banking Information Infrastructure Committee and the Financial Services Sector Coordinating Council's AI Executive Oversight Group, these resources aim to establish common terminology and risk management practices for AI deployment in financial services.
key_points: U.S. Treasury released two AI guidance resources: an AI Lexicon and the Financial Services AI Risk Management Framework (FS AI RMF)
Documents were developed through the Financial and Banking Information Committee and AI Executive Oversight Group (AIEOG)
The AI Lexicon establishes common definitions for AI concepts, capabilities, and risk categories to improve governance and oversight
The FS AI RMF adapts the NIST AI Risk Management Framework to financial services operational and regulatory considerations
Resources are designed to be scalable for institutions of varying size and complexity
Release supports implementation of the President's AI Action Plan through public-private collaboration
why_it_matters: This source is regulatory guidance on AI governance, not a macro catalyst or market structure content. It does not discuss interest rates, monetary policy, economic data releases, or market-moving events. It is relevant only to firms directly implementing AI systems who need compliance frameworks.
